Contra Costa County ( ; Contra Costa, Spanish for 'Opposite Coast') is a county located in the U.S. state of California, in the East Bay of the San Francisco Bay Area.

description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: California wine region in Alameda County
description_wiki: Livermore Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) in Alameda County, California, centered around the city of Livermore in the Tri-Valley region which is composed of Amador, San Ramon, and Livermore valleys.

description: city and state in the North of Germany
description_wiki: Hamburg, officially the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, is the second-largest city in Germany after Berlin and seventh-largest city in the European Union, with a population of over 1.9 million.

description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: 1893 World's Fair held in Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: The World's Columbian Exposition, also known as the Chicago World's Fair, was a world's fair held in Chicago  from May 1 to October 31, 1893, to celebrate the 400th anniversary of Christopher Columbus's arrival in the New World in 1492.
Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

Schleswig-Holstein (; German: [ˌʃleːsvɪç ˈhɔlʃtaɪn] ; Danish: Slesvig-Holsten [ˌsle̝ːsvi ˈhʌlˌste̝ˀn]; Low German: Sleswig-Holsteen; North Frisian: Slaswik-Holstiinj; occasionally in English Sleswick-Holsatia) is the northernmost of the 16 states of Germany, comprising most of the historical Duchy of Holstein and the southern part of the former Duchy of Schleswig.

description: country in South America and Oceania, with a claim in Antarctica
description_wiki: Chile, officially the Republic of Chile, is a country in western South America.
